questions.question_text: Under ORC 4735.081, every Ohio brokerage must designate which person to oversee operations?
distractors: A managing salesperson
A compliance officer
A designated supervisor
questions.explanation: ORC 4735.081 requires each brokerage to designate a principal broker who oversees and directs brokerage operations, including compliance, trust accounts, and licensee supervision.

questions.question_text: An Ohio principal broker delegates trust account management to a salesperson. The account is later found to have errors. Who is responsible?
distractors: Only the salesperson who managed the account
Both equally with no hierarchy
The Division of Real Estate directly
questions.explanation: Even when trust account management is delegated, the principal broker retains ultimate responsibility. The broker must ensure proper handling regardless of who performs the day-to-day management.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a principal broker responsibility under Ohio law?
questions.correct_answer: Setting commission rates for the local MLS
distractors: Maintaining brokerage compliance with license law
Supervising affiliated salespersons
Ensuring the fair housing sign is displayed
questions.explanation: Commission rates are freely negotiable and not set by any single broker for the MLS. Principal brokers are responsible for compliance, supervision, trust accounts, and office displays.

questions.question_text: A principal broker fails to display the required fair housing sign in the brokerage office. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of Ohio license law requirements
distractors: Only a federal fair housing violation
Not a violation unless a complaint is filed
A recommended practice but not mandatory
questions.explanation: Ohio law requires brokers to display an equal housing opportunity sign prominently in their office. Failure to do so is a compliance violation the principal broker is responsible for.

questions.question_text: A principal broker discovers that one of their salespersons has been advertising without including the brokerage name. What is the broker's obligation?
questions.correct_answer: Take corrective action and ensure compliance
distractors: File a complaint with the Division themselves
Terminate the salesperson immediately
Pay a fine on behalf of the salesperson
questions.explanation: The principal broker is responsible for supervising affiliated licensees and ensuring compliance. They must take corrective action to stop the violation and prevent future occurrences.

questions.question_text: An Ohio broker and salesperson have an independent contractor agreement. Who is responsible for the salesperson's real estate activities?
questions.correct_answer: The broker, regardless of the agreement type
distractors: The salesperson as an independent contractor
Both share equal legal responsibility
Neither, if the agreement disclaims liability
questions.explanation: Regardless of whether the relationship is employer-employee or independent contractor, the broker retains responsibility for the salesperson's licensed real estate activities.

questions.question_text: Which two types of working relationships can exist between an Ohio broker and salesperson?
distractors: Partner or associate only
Franchise or subsidiary only
Intern or apprentice only
questions.explanation: Ohio allows broker-salesperson relationships as either employer-employee or independent contractor. The classification affects tax treatment but not the broker's supervisory responsibility.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed Ohio salesperson joins a brokerage as an independent contractor. What training obligation does the broker have?
questions.correct_answer: The broker must provide adequate training
distractors: No training is required for independent contractors
Only the 20-hour post-license course counts
Training is optional for experienced hires only
questions.explanation: Regardless of the employment relationship, the broker must provide adequate training and supervision to all affiliated licensees, including independent contractors.

questions.question_text: The independent contractor status of a salesperson primarily affects which aspect of the broker-salesperson relationship?
distractors: The broker's supervisory responsibilities
Whether the salesperson can show property
The salesperson's trust account access
questions.explanation: The independent contractor vs. employee distinction primarily affects tax treatment. It does not change the broker's responsibility for supervising the salesperson's real estate activities.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's independent contractor agreement states the broker is 'not liable for the salesperson's actions.' Is this enforceable under Ohio law?
questions.correct_answer: No, brokers cannot disclaim supervisory liability
distractors: Yes, written agreements are binding
Yes, for independent contractors only
Only if both parties sign and notarize
questions.explanation: Under Ohio law, the broker retains responsibility for a salesperson's real estate activities regardless of contractual disclaimers. This supervisory duty cannot be contracted away.

questions.question_text: A sole broker dies unexpectedly while several transactions are pending. What Ohio mechanism protects the clients?
questions.correct_answer: An ancillary trustee may be appointed
distractors: The Division takes over all transactions
The salespersons continue independently
All pending transactions are automatically voided
questions.explanation: Under ORC 4735.081, when a sole broker dies, is suspended, or has their license revoked, an ancillary trustee can be appointed by the Superintendent to conclude pending business.

questions.question_text: Who qualifies the ancillary trustee to conclude pending business when an Ohio broker becomes unavailable?
distractors: The Ohio Real Estate Commission
The deceased broker's family
The local Board of Realtors
questions.explanation: The Superintendent of Real Estate qualifies the ancillary trustee. This person then has authority to conclude pending transactions to protect clients.

questions.question_text: Under which circumstances can an ancillary trustee be appointed in Ohio?
questions.correct_answer: Sole broker dies, is suspended, or is revoked
distractors: Any time a broker goes on extended vacation
When a broker's CE is not completed on time
Only when the broker files for bankruptcy
questions.explanation: An ancillary trustee is appointed when a sole broker (with no other broker in the firm) dies, has their license revoked, or is suspended — situations leaving no one to manage active business.

questions.question_text: What is the primary role of an ancillary trustee in Ohio?
distractors: Take new listings for the brokerage
Distribute the broker's personal assets
Hire replacement salespersons for the firm
questions.explanation: The ancillary trustee's purpose is to protect clients by concluding transactions already in progress. They do not take on new business or manage the brokerage long-term.

questions.question_text: A brokerage has two licensed brokers. One broker dies. Is an ancillary trustee needed?
questions.correct_answer: No, the remaining broker can manage the business
distractors: Yes, both brokers must be replaced
Yes, but only for 90 days
Only if the deceased broker was the principal broker
questions.explanation: The ancillary trustee provision applies only when there is no other broker in the brokerage. If another broker remains, they can manage operations and conclude pending business.
